methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.1B - $250.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: xAI

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $3.75B

private.name: WEKA

private.name: Modal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $1.1B

data.narrative:
SandboxAQ is a Series E artificial intelligence and software company founded by Jack Hidary, currently valued at $5.6B post-money following $500M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 5 same-stage, same-sector private comparables (xAI, Crusoe Energy, Ayar Labs, WEKA, Modal) and 1 public reference (Palantir), reflecting the fragmented landscape of frontier AI infrastructure.

Two triangulation methods yield materially different perspectives. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier to the $5.6B last post-money valuation, implying $7.8B at the next funding stage. Peer-median methodology across the 5 private comparables yields $3.8B, though the dispersion is extreme—ranging from $1.1B (Modal) to $250.0B (xAI)—making the median more reliable than any single point estimate. The xAI outlier and $1.6B–$10.0B clustering of other peers indicate heterogeneous risk profiles within frontier AI.

Triangulating these methods produces a $3.2B–$9.0B range with a median of $5.8B. The current $5.6B valuation sits near the midpoint, suggesting fair positioning relative to stage and peer set. Key uncertainties center on whether SandboxAQ's business model, unit economics, and product-market fit align with high-valuation peers (Crusoe, Ayar Labs in the $3.8B–$10.0B band) or the lower quartile. The extreme peer dispersion limits confidence in any precise point valuation.
